personal: Studied classical trumpet at the University of Toronto and spent years pursuing a music career in New York before pivoting to tech sales at companies like Yelp, Groupon, Taboola, and GumGum. Met co-founder Peter Spradling at Antler NY's inaugural residency in 2019. Originally from Toronto, Canada.
newHooks: *Classical trumpet degree from U of T
Enterprise sales at two unicorns (Taboola, GumGum)

personal: Grew up on a farm in southern Brazil. Lost his father around age 19-20, which prompted a career shift -- switched to night classes while working full-time and became international treasurer of a Brazilian multinational by 21-22. His grandfather advised him that the farm wasn't big enough for a prosperous career and he was 'too talented to dream small.' Avid skier with a skiing experience he says he'll never forget.
newHooks: *Grew up on a farm in southern Brazil
*Lost father at 19, became corporate treasurer by 21

personal: Played ice hockey throughout childhood and college, and still plays as an adult. Supports youth hockey leagues in the Miami area. Also an avid skier. Before entering finance, built a retail dry cleaning and garment restoration business that grossed nearly $10M/year. Started on the Chicago Board Options Exchange in 1993.
No personal details found in public sources. Over a decade of experience in financial services. Drives strategic vision and operational excellence at PIRS Capital.

One of nine children. Grandparents are Holocaust survivors. Graduated from Yeshiva Gedolah of Greater Detroit; bachelor's in accounting from Wayne State University. Co-founded a New York-based tax preparation company at age 19, which he sold. Passionate about criminal justice reform -- launched a program to employ people convicted of misdemeanors or nonviolent felonies, paying for their transportation and court fines during a 30-day placement.
newHooks: *One of nine children, grandparents are Holocaust survivors
*Sold a tax prep company at age 19

personal: Numbers have been a passion since childhood. Attended chess clubs as a kid and applies strategic thinking from chess to business. Previously engineered and sold proprietary fintech technology before founding FundKite. Emphasizes reading body language to make smart decisions.
newHooks: Built and sold proprietary fintech tech before FundKite
Reads body language as a business skill

personal: No personal details found in public sources beyond professional background. Based in Baltimore, Maryland. Has appeared on multiple fintech podcasts including Tearsheet and PayPod discussing small business lending.

Holds degrees in psychology and political science from Touro College. Enjoys travel, cultural experiences, and playing basketball with his children. Forbes Finance Council member. Also Co-CEO of Bizcap with global operations spanning the US, UK, Australia, and Singapore.
newHooks: Psychology and political science background (Touro College)
Global operator: US, UK, Australia, Singapore

personal: Based in Portsmouth, New Hampshire. Saint Anselm College graduate with a BA in Sociology focused on Human Relations in Work. Over 22 years in equipment leasing and small business lending. Previously CRO at CAN Capital where he revitalized a legacy brand.

Florida State University alumnus (Business Administration and Finance). Started in credit card processing in 2007, launched Greenbox Capital around 2012. Active community volunteer and passionate Humane Society volunteer. Expanded into the Canadian market in 2016.
newHooks: Started in credit card processing at age ~20

personal: U.S. Marine Corps veteran who draws on military leadership principles. His father, Jim Crawford, was the former president of the Harness Horse Breeders of New York State. Albert and his wife Michelle own and operate Crawford Farms, breeding harness racing horses alongside running BHG. Grew up learning to provide for those less fortunate. Started career at the New York Futures Exchange in 1984. Named Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year for Financial Services and selected for Inc. magazine's CEO Project.
newHooks: *Breeds harness racing horses at Crawford Farms
*Father was president of Harness Horse Breeders of NY
Started at NY Futures Exchange in 1984

personal: Married with three children (Scarlett, John Connor, and Savannah). He and his wife believe that 'to whom much is given, much is required' and actively give back through children's causes in their community. Has been an entrepreneur for over half his life, founded ACA Financial LLC in 1999 before One Park Financial in 2010. Credits his father's influence for his entrepreneurial path.
newHooks: *Father of three, active in children's causes
*'To whom much is given, much is required' philosophy
Entrepreneur for over half his life

personal: University of Miami Herbert Business School graduate (BBA). Personal mission is to make low-cost SBA financing accessible to the smallest of small businesses. Founded iBusiness Funding in 2015, which developed the technology powering Knight Capital's growth into a top-10 SBA 7(a) small balance lending program.
newHooks: University of Miami business school grad
Mission-driven: SBA access for smallest businesses

personal: Raised on a farm in rural Idaho. Self-taught serial entrepreneur who started seven businesses in 20 years, beginning with a sign repair shop at 22 after getting rejected for his first business loan -- the experience that planted the seeds for Nav. At 26, a brutal 360-degree review where his whole company gave him one-star reviews on communication transformed him from boss to leader. Speaks openly about mental health and panic attacks, advocating for psychological safety in leadership.
newHooks: *Openly advocates for mental health in leadership
First business loan rejection inspired Nav

personal: Wanted to be a hairstylist, lawyer, teacher, and medical professional as a kid before being hired at a private equity firm out of college where 'everything clicked.' Married 13+ years to a husband in law enforcement. Describes herself as a 'modern soccer mom' -- her oldest son plays goalie in the MLS Next program with Jacksonville FC, which keeps the family traveling throughout the Southeast. Self-described introvert. Dream job would be singing, but she's tone deaf. Loves concerts and has eclectic music taste.
newHooks: *Son plays goalie in MLS Next (Jacksonville FC)

personal: Based between Miami and Central America, spending 50% of his time traveling between the two plus New York and Texas. Started Lendinero in 2013 with less than $1,000 after being inspired by applying online for a personal loan. His wife Yahaira Pichardo helped him restructure operations in Nicaragua. Harvard Business School executive education graduate. Bachelor's and post-graduate studies from FIU and Florida State. Featured in Forbes, Entrepreneur Magazine, and Miami Herald.
newHooks: Lives between Miami and Central America
Wife Yahaira helped rebuild Nicaraguan operations

personal: Family emigrated from Colombia to Miami in the late 1980s; raised in Miami with an entrepreneurial upbringing. Cornell University business school graduate. Worked in financial services in New York City and in economic research for the federal government in Washington D.C. before founding GoKapital. Guest lectures to FIU business students on entrepreneurship.
newHooks: Guest lectures at FIU on entrepreneurship

personal: No personal details found in public sources. Baruch College (CUNY) graduate. Has held roles at L3 Funding and Gemini Business Funding in the financial services industry.

Grew up in Hartford, Connecticut, in a tough environment with drugs, gangs, and crime. Met his father for the first time around age 13. Previously founded Kanopy Streetwear before moving to Miami to enter the MCA industry and launching Uplyft Capital in 2012. Also hosts the 'Small Business Stories' podcast featuring entrepreneurs who built success from the bottom.
newHooks: *Grew up in tough Hartford, CT neighborhood
*Met his father for the first time at age 13

personal: Family business -- runs Westwood Funding with his brother Anthony. Great-grandfather was Rabbi Jacob Avigdor from Poland, who was the chief rabbi of his town and was arrested in 1943 for being Jewish. Has provided over $1 billion in funding to small and middle-market businesses.
newHooks: *Great-grandfather was a chief rabbi arrested in 1943 Poland
